title: No Loathsomeness In Love.
content:
What I fancy I approve,
    No dislike there is in love.
    Be my mistress short or tall,
    And distorted therewithal:
    Be she likewise one of those
    That an acre hath of nose:
    Be her forehead and her eyes
    Full of incongruities:
    Be her cheeks so shallow too
    As to show her tongue wag through;
    Be her lips ill hung or set,
    And her grinders black as jet:
    Has she thin hair, hath she none,
    She's to me a paragon.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "No Loathsomeness In Love.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
